channel.title: Comments for page "Different Kinds of Nihilism"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Different Kinds of Nihilism"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why I upvote this Tale just now ? I don't know.

Did I enjoy this ? Totally.

Did the Gamers are still some of the coolest characters of the lore ? Abso-fucking-lutely.

Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the whole wiki is considered scifi, the entire Foundation universe is, and I figured that applies to everything on it

Nah, we've definitely got plenty of stuff from other genres. For example, so much straight-up fantasy stuff that the sister site the Wanderers' Library was born from it.

Furthermore, despite scifi having heavy political topics, it doesn't NEED them, and I don't think it really belongs in the SCP universe.

Totally an understandable opinion to have! That said, I don't think it's possible for everyone on the site to agree with exactly what belongs in the Foundation-verse, so if you're more likely to enjoy articles that don't have as much political content, maybe check out some categories in the User Curated Lists? http://www.scp-wiki.net/user-curated-lists

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't say GAW was supposed to be scifi, the whole wiki is considered scifi, the entire Foundation universe is, and I figured that applies to everything on it. GAW really doesn't delve enough into the "sci" half of that, as you pointed out, and that's always been my biggest problem with them. I never really complained though because I liked the memetic quality of the GOI.

But hey, what do I know, I'm not exactly a site veteran.

Furthermore, despite scifi having heavy political topics, it doesn't NEED them, and I don't think it really belongs in the SCP universe. Plus, it generally just divides people and causes problems, so i don't think it's good for community cohesion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That guy could use a hug, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the big man….
HASS the half of the alien intelligence

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't think of any Gamers Against Weed content that could be described as "science fiction". Nothing is given much of a scientific treatment. It's magical realism, maybe urban fantasy as well.

Moreover, even if this WAS science fiction, that genre has a long and storied tradition of both A) being heavily political in-universe, and B) functioning as social commentary out-of-universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really a fan of the political side of GAW, mostly because it's a little too real for something that's supposed to be sci-fi.

That being said, this is pretty good. No vote, but good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed, for a variety of reasons. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Don't make a pun
- Too late

You mean Anomalous?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ You could say they are
- I am so sorry for the pun

an anomalous Anonymous?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good shit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. I think it's a pretty accurate portrayal of what goes on with an individual behind the computer screen. I think GAW draws parallels with Anonymous. You have some individuals that want to do good. You have other individuals who are just in it for the lulz. But they're all united by common interest and (some) desire for human interaction. The characterization of bluntfiend shows this pretty well.

+1. We need more stories in an internet setting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can confirm, chat mods are unfeeling queer eldritch destroyers who enjoy homestuck.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is the most heartwarming thing I've ever read on this site, goddamn. +1, Gamers Against Weed for best GOI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the fire rises

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm very dumb in the mornings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am so sorry, my dude, for messing up who wrote this!

Anyways how dare you. I cried. I literally cried. I had to go to class after reading this and do you know how hard it is to focus on anthropology when someone just strangled you with your own emotions? Pretty damn hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so glad I read this comment because I thought there were like, two Kalinins at one point or that I was going crazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but you started the fire

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I really liked the classic AWCY stuff, even when some people were overusing it. It had a certain style to it.

And I really like this too, for a lot of the same reasons. Keep up the good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well my bitterness about not being able to do Mr. Original Character doesn't prevent me from enjoying the hell out of this and upvoting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might be difficult to find most of them, because they're under a deleted account, but they are definitely there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hello, I did this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … I didn't write this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*unholy shrieking*

Scantron how dare you garrote me with my own heartstrings

Edit: I just re-read this and

bones: Bluntfiend, may I ask you a question after this initial question?

How many times have they pulled "you just did" on poor 2721?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't walk into this realizing it was going to be GOW, but I enjoyed it thoroughly as the revelations came. 2721 was especially fun.

A hearty +1

I definitely see the issue with painting them a a reaction to AWCY?, even if that's the heart of this particular character's tale rather than that of the organization. I know they also enjoy taking the piss out of Wondertainment.

I think this is a good step towards characterizing them, especially including implications of what Bones is doing there in the first place. I also think the misters so far are good at conveying more character-driven stories than "anti-GOI snark fest" or even the "Wondertainment-is-nuts" story that drives his little misters, but that targeted-snark-pile is the pitfall to avoid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn, that emotional impact surprised me. Also GAW is becoming one of my favorite GOIs. GG

+1

Also SCP-2721 is surprisingly fitting as a part of GAW

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh phew, because I sure as hell did. (also I can't not upvote this with my username, even if it wasn't as fantastically written as it is +1).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Search around, you'll find them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding much of this, but in a more enthusiastic fashion.

I'm loving GAW, and I think this piece in particular solidifies for me a big reason why: They're existentialists1. Nihilists stare into the abyss and see fear. Existentialists see a blank canvas.

The next step in their growth needs to be making them more than just a foil to AWCY, and this piece sets up an avenue for that growth rather nicely. While both in and out of universe their beginnings are a band of merry pranksters, their real potential lies in the same sort of nebulous, "smiling punk" zone as Anonymous- that odd place somewhere between a band of vigilantes with a vaguely noble cause and a bunch of cackling trolls looking for an excuse. There's a lot of depth to explore there, I think.

Side note: Eli and GAW are perfect together. Nice that it's found a place to belong. X3

Footnotes
1. aka anti-nihilists

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
because I've banged on the anti-GoI drum enough that I think my thoughts on that are well-known at this point

I've never heard your drum-bangings about that. What exactly do you find wrong with them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've tried to stay out of commenting on this GoI, mostly because I've banged on the anti-GoI drum enough that I think my thoughts on that are well-known at this point, and also because a lot of my reaction is on a personal level of distaste.

This is probably the best of the GAW articles, in the sense that it actually has some reason to exist and is saying something beyond the "look I am repeating this meme" sensibilities of pretty much everything else to come out of this. I was hesitant to downvote it for that reason, because if this group has to exist, it is far more palatable in this iteration and I don't really want to discourage that. In the end, though, I'm really unlikely to be moved that much by IRC conversations between teenagers and someone that's still pretty new to the "disillusionment about the world" game.

One thing that I really haven't cared for is the development, whether intended or as a reaction to the commentary surrounding these articles, of this group as an anti-AWCY? anartist option. People fixate on AWCY? like their problem was pointless destruction and hipsterism, when what the problem has usually been is the promotion of art as a conceptual sledgehammer what teaches you lessons. The best art can be political, it can have a clear message, but usually its distinguishing feature is not how quickly you can lead a patron from Point A to Point B. This piece recognizes that, to an extent, but that understanding is pretty diluted with all the "when will the killing end" business that is really beside the point here.

Mostly, though? Even art viewed through an anti-AWCY? lens is still art placed along the same tired, shallow, puerile axis that is set by the existence of AWCY?. Defining yourself in opposition to something is still giving that thing a large degree of power over the conversation. I don't really see a whole lot of potential in the core concept in any sense, to be honest, but shackling this to AWCY?, like it needs some sort of in-universe repudiation before anyone can finally just write about art without weird hangups, doesn't strike me as a sustainable model or one that's going to lead to anything except a continuation of the endless and unproductive fixation on AWCY?.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a lot, and I mean that in the best way. It did a lot to develop GAW and what they're about, differentiated them from AWCY? which is good, because I was worried about GAW becoming the lolrandom version of AWCY?, and those fears have been thoroughly dashed, with their similarities being nothing more than superficial as of this tale. Bones being Eli is just really cool to me, and it warmed this old homestuck's heart to see GAW's reaction to him.

But most of all, what I really liked about this tale is how heartfelt and real the changes in bluntfiend's life, emotions, and values over the last eight years felt. I related to it on a personal level, as I'm sure many of us do, and that on its own is worthy of an upvote.

+1, pakoosh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still not sure about the long-term potential of shitposters/the Merry Pranksters as a GOI, but I have enjoyed all of what I have seen for various reasons. Also there's the fact that I'm pretty much at exact sweet spot that Mr. xXxB1uN7F13NDxXx's monologue resonated very particularly for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been reading articles on this site for at least 4 years and only started interacting recently. In that time I've read stories that made me laugh, ones that made a chill run down my spine and ones that made me feel warm inside but I don't think that I've ever come across one that truly upset me.

But this? A story about a disillusioned weed head who has had enough of hurting and killing people and how he's starting to come back from a dark place? This pretty much has me in tears. Thank you for the experience. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still think AWCY gets the short end in these but ah well. Already gave critique in private. I enjoy this. Only explicit GAW thing I enjoy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cut off one head, and two more shall arise.

Great work.

E: according to my calculationsa searchthis marks the goi-readiness point for gamers.

In gawspeak:

u deed eeeeet

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe there's more room in my heart for another GAW thing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: REVENGE IS MINE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fuck you first posting son of a fuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This might have come more from the heart than someone would expect. This is a story about how people change and their values change, and what's really important. GAW, to me, comes from a political place, and that's where I wanted to go to in this tale. I hope you like it.

And don't worry, I don't have any plans to write any more GAW anytime soon.

Edit: I also do not have any ideas for Mr. Bernie Sanders. Bluntfiend does, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really digging all this characterization of GAW you're doing, kinch — it's as much yours as it is mine at this point, probably moreso. I love all of these characters, and I love this backstory for the whole thing. Good showing all-around.
